questions.questionText: A resistor has a potential difference of 6V across it and carries a current of 2A. What is its resistance?
questions.hint: V = IR. Rearrange to find R.

questions.questionText: Three resistors of 2Ω, 3Ω, and 5Ω are connected in series. What is the total resistance?
Two resistors of 6Ω and 3Ω are connected in parallel. What is their combined resistance?
A battery of EMF 12V and internal resistance 1Ω is connected to a 5Ω resistor. What is the terminal voltage?
questions.hint: First find the current: I = EMF / (R + r). Then V = EMF - Ir.

questions.questionText: The power dissipated in a 4Ω resistor carrying 3A is:
In a parallel circuit, which quantity is the same across all branches?
questions.hint: Think about what it means to be connected between the same two nodes.
questions.explanation: All branches in a parallel circuit are connected between the same two points, so they all experience the same potential difference (voltage).

questions.questionText: A 60W bulb operates at 240V. What is its resistance?
questions.hint: P = V²/R. Rearrange to find R.

questions.questionText: What is the terminal voltage of a battery when no current is flowing (open circuit)?
questions.hint: V = EMF - Ir. What is Ir when I = 0?
questions.explanation: When I = 0, V = EMF - (0 × r) = EMF. The terminal voltage equals the EMF when no current flows.

questions.questionText: A potential divider uses resistors of 2Ω and 8Ω connected to a 10V supply. What is the voltage across the 8Ω resistor?
Two identical bulbs are connected in parallel. If one bulb is removed, what happens to the brightness of the remaining bulb?
questions.hint: What voltage does each bulb receive in a parallel circuit?
questions.explanation: In parallel, each bulb receives the full supply voltage regardless of the other. Removing one does not change the voltage across the remaining bulb, so its brightness is unchanged.
